prerequisites: A Google Ads manager account (MCC). A manager account is required to request API access; the developer token is generated from its API Center.

An approved Google Ads API developer token, obtained from the API Center at ads.google.com/aw/apicenter by completing the API Access form and accepting the Terms and Conditions. The token is a 22-character alphanumeric string. New tokens are granted Explorer Access by default; Basic or Standard access is required to remove those restrictions for full production use.
Google OAuth 2.0 client credentials (client ID and client secret) created in a Google Cloud project, used to authorize access to a Google Ads account.
An OAuth 2.0 refresh token generated by authorizing the OAuth client against the Google Ads account to be read.
The 10-digit Google Ads customer ID of the account to query, entered without hyphens (the optional customer_id config field).

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0 with developer token
authMethods.summary: The connector authenticates with Google OAuth 2.0 plus a developer token, matching the secret fields credentials.client_secret, credentials.refresh_token, credentials.access_token, and credentials.developer_token. OAuth 2.0 lets the client access a user's Google Ads account without handling or storing the user's login info; the client_secret and refresh_token are used to mint short-lived access tokens. In addition to the OAuth credentials, every API request requires the developer token, which uniquely identifies the API caller and is sent in the developer-token request header.

setupSteps.title: Create or select a Google Ads manager account
setupSteps.body: Sign in to Google Ads with a manager account (MCC). A manager account is required to request API access. If you do not have one, create it using an email not already tied to an existing Google Ads account.

setupSteps.title: Request a developer token in the API Center
setupSteps.body: Go to the API Center at ads.google.com/aw/apicenter, complete the API Access form, and accept the Terms and Conditions. Copy the generated 22-character developer token. New tokens are granted Explorer Access by default; apply for Basic or Standard access to remove restrictions for production use. Use this value for credentials.developer_token.

setupSteps.title: Create OAuth 2.0 client credentials
setupSteps.body: In a Google Cloud project, create an OAuth 2.0 client ID and client secret for the Google Ads API. The client secret maps to credentials.client_secret. OAuth 2.0 enables the connector to access a user's Google Ads account without handling or storing the user's login info.

setupSteps.title: Generate a refresh token
setupSteps.body: Authorize the OAuth 2.0 client against the Google Ads account you want to read to obtain a refresh token (credentials.refresh_token). The connector exchanges the refresh token for short-lived access tokens (credentials.access_token) when calling the API.

setupSteps.title: Configure the account and sync settings
setupSteps.body: Provide the 10-digit customer ID without hyphens in the customer_id field to target the account to read. Optionally set start_date and end_date to bound the reporting window, conversion_window_days for the conversion lookback, customer_status_filter to restrict which customers are synced, custom_queries_array for additional GAQL queries, and num_workers to tune parallelism.
